notes: Soak corms 4-8 hrs before planting. Cut when buds show color but not fully open for 10+ day vase life. Zone 6b: dig and store corms after first frost, or mulch heavily in ground.
Direct sow March-April. Needs trellis or support. Incredible fragrance. Cut when first bud opens.
Cut when 1/3 of bottom florets open. Vase life 7-10 days. Pinch at 4 inches for bushier plants. Succession sow every 3-4 weeks.
Large azalea-type blooms on tall sturdy stems. Vase life 7-10 days. Cool-weather flower - strongest in spring and fall.
Plant bulbs 6 inches deep in November. Cut when color shows in bud. Vase life 5-7 days. Naturalizes well.
Plant bulbs 6 inches deep in November. Vase life 6-10 days. Keep daffodils in their own vase - their sap shortens other flowers vase life.
The workhorse. 4-6 inch fully double blooms on long sturdy stems. Vase life 7-10 days. Direct sow after last frost.
Stunning chartreuse-to-rose coloring. Vase life up to 14 days. Lime-green center sets off everything in a bouquet.
Classic cut flower mix. 1.5-2.5 inch double and semi-double blooms. Prolific and reliable. Succession sow every 3 weeks.
Double and semi-double blooms. Nearly carefree. Vase life 7-10 days. Cut when petals flat open, center disk visible. Drought tolerant.
Soft peachy-apricot coloring that lightens as blooms mature. Vase life 7-10 days. Excellent for wedding and event work.
Pure white single blooms on tall graceful stems. Vase life 7-10 days. Mixes beautifully with everything.
Single stem cut flower - one bloom per plant. Pollenless so no mess. Vase life 7-12 days. Succession sow every 2 weeks.
Double fluffy blooms - fuzzy golden. Dwarf 18-30 inches. Vase life 6-12 days. Branching plant produces multiple blooms.
Deep burgundy-red branching sunflower. Multiple blooms per plant. Vase life 7-12 days. Stunning in mixed bouquets.
Iconic wedding flower - 8-10 inch dinner plate blooms in blush/cream/peach. Vase life 5-7 days. Cut at 3/4 open. Needs staking. Dig tubers after first frost.
Deep rich purple, 8-inch dinner plate blooms. Vase life 5-7 days. Stunning in arrangements. Dig and store tubers after first frost.
Single blooms with dark burgundy-black foliage. Vase life 5-7 days. Luminous orange-red flowers contrast beautifully with dark foliage.
Queen of cut flowers. Vase life 2-3 WEEKS. Cut when 1-2 blooms open, rest still buds. Slow - start indoors 10-12 weeks before last frost. Zone 6b blooms mid-July through frost.
Strong stems, semi-double to double blooms. Vase life 2-3 weeks. Similar to Echo Mix.
Excellent dried flower - indefinite vase life when dried. Cut when fully developed and hang upside down to dry. Also beautiful fresh. Drought tolerant.
Rich midnight blue-purple. Stunning dried or fresh. Cut when fully colored and hang to dry. Indefinite life when dried.
Dusty mauve and bronze tones. Vase life 7-10 days. Blooms first year from seed. Perennial - cut back after hard frost.
Classic golden yellow double blooms. Vase life 7-10 days. Divide every 3-4 years in spring.
Unique quilled and twisted petals in warm rust and orange tones. Vase life 7-10 days. Distinctive textural element.
One of the best fall cut flowers. Blooms August-October when everything else is winding down. Vase life 7-14 days.
Bright blue-violet blooms in fall. Compact and free-flowering. Vase life 7-14 days.
Deep red-purple fall blooms. Compact plant. Vase life 7-14 days. One of the earliest asters to bloom.
Stunning fall color - fiery red-orange-gold. Velvety cockscomb blooms. Vase life 2+ weeks fresh, indefinitely dried.
Intense dark purple plumes. Vase life 2+ weeks fresh, indefinite dried. Heat and drought tolerant.
Plumed cockscomb type in a color mix. Vase life 2+ weeks fresh, indefinitely dried.
Classic dried flower. Cut and hang upside down to dry - never needs water once dried. Indefinite life.
Lacy elegant filler - dark purple, pink, and white umbels. Vase life 7-10 days. Self-sows freely. Stunning with Cafe au Lait dahlias.
Deep purple ornamental basil. Smells amazing. Cut stems before flower buds open for best vase life (7-10 days). Bonus herb harvest.
Grandiflora rose. Bicolor - golden-orange inside, bronzy-red outside. Continuous blooms. Strong cutting stems, excellent vase life. Very disease-resistant.
Proven Winners 2022 Rose of the Year. Red-orange buds open to soft orange fading to golden yellow. Self-cleaning, no deadheading. Exceptionally disease-resistant.
Russells grows dahlias in many colors. Ask staff for what is in stock. Zone 6b: treat as annual, or dig and store tubers after first frost.
Russells grows zinnia transplants in many colors. Great for filling gaps. Full sun, well-drained soil. Pinch to encourage branching.
Russells carries snapdragon transplants in a range of colors. Ask what is currently in stock.
Russells carries Jacob Cline bee balm - deep scarlet red, mildew resistant, attracts hummingbirds.
Russells carries black-eyed Susan varieties. Classic native perennial. Blooms July-September.
Russells carries echinacea in many varieties. Native prairie plant. Blooms June-August.
Roses available from Russell. Ask staff for current varieties and colors in stock.
